description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a bright orange fuselage and wings, featuring two stacked wings connected by struts and wires. The nose houses a wooden propeller and an exposed radial engine, while the undercarriage consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with visible control panels and a single seat. The tail section is painted with a colorful geometric pattern and includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The surface of the airplane appears to be made of metal and wood, with fabric covering the wings. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, suitable for display or operation in a historical or recreational urban context.
description: A vintage orange biplane with an open cockpit, wooden propeller, and colorful tail, featuring stacked wings and exposed engine details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the orange fuselage, stacked wings, open cockpit, and colorful tail fin.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wooden propeller, exposed engine, and symmetrical biplane wing structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the tail's geometric pattern and the biplane's wing struts.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, colorful vertical stabilizer, and cockpit instrumentation.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by vertical struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed cylinders and a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers; colorful pattern on the vertical fin.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage, with visible control panel and no canopy.
